title: Hut 8 Mining Corp. Q2 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Hut 8 Q2 2024 Earnings Summary. Revenue growth: 72% year-over-year to $35.2 million. Net loss: $71.9 million (vs. $1.7 million loss in Q2 2023). Adjusted EBITDA: Loss of $57.5 million (vs. gain of $14.8 million prior year). Substantial losses driven by $71.8 million loss on digital assets from Bitcoin price decline (March 31: $71,289 to June 30: $62,668). Digital asset mining gross margins: Expanded from 34% to 46% year-over-year. Power cost: $0.032 per kilowatt hour (40% reduction QoQ). Mining cost: Increased 7% post-halving to $26,232 per Bitcoin. Balance Sheet: Cash $175.5 million, Bitcoin holdings 9,102 BTC valued at $570.5 million, Total debt $328.8 million (excluding leases). Received $150 million convertible note from Coatue Management. Power Infrastructure: 1,322 MW total power footprint, 205 MW greenfield site in Texas Panhandle expected energization first half 2025, 268 MW converted since merger. Fleet Upgrade: Active negotiations underway for manufacturer partnerships and project financing. AI/HPC Expansion: GPU-as-a-Service vertical expected launch Q3 2024, Multi-100 MW AI projects in active discussions, Texas Panhandle site could support 205 MW of Nvidia Blackwell GPUs.
